acceptance_criteria: `archivist` agent prompt and registration land in the standard agent location used by `research-assistant` (AB#3133).
Output schema is declared once and referenced by both the writer step (task-3) and the sub-workflow routing (task-2).
`expand` decisions populate `refined_research_needs` matching AB#3141's payload schema; `keep` decisions populate `title` and `johnny_decimal_hint`; `discard` requires only `rationale`.
Unit-level tests cover schema validation for all three dispositions (well-formed and malformed).
description: Define the `archivist` agent and freeze its structured output schema: `{disposition: keep|discard|expand, rationale, title?, johnny_decimal_hint?, refined_research_needs?}`. The agent reads the synthesized findings document plus the scratch artefact path and returns exactly one decision. Schema must be stable — it is the contract consumed by task-3 (writer) and by sibling AB#3137's downstream validators. No workflow wiring in this task; agent + schema + unit-level prompt validation only.
title: `archivist` agent definition + decision schema

acceptance_criteria: Every successful path through the research sub-workflow terminates in exactly one archivist invocation.
`discard` exits cleanly with no writer invocation.
`expand` re-enters the sub-workflow with the refined `research_needs` payload; the re-entry honours a configurable expansion cap (default 1) to prevent runaway loops.
`keep` hands off to the writer step (task-3) with the archivist's decision + the findings document attached.
Harness scenario covers terminal disposition routing for all three branches (writer can be stubbed at this layer).
description: Append the archivist as the terminal stage of the research sub-workflow (built in AB#3133). Insertion is at the structurally terminal end of the YAML step list — disjoint from AB#3134's mid-loop escalation insertion. Add routing: `discard` → terminal no-op; `keep` → writer step (task-3); `expand` → re-enter the sub-workflow with `refined_research_needs` (subject to the same escalation cap policy from AB#3134, default 1 expansion per topic to mirror the deep-tier cap).
title: Wire `archivist` as terminal step in research sub-workflow

acceptance_criteria: Writer accepts an archivist `keep` decision + findings document and produces one article via the AB#3132 storage abstraction.
Path follows Johnny-Decimal convention deterministically given the same hint + INDEX state.
Frontmatter contains `title`, `captured_at` (ISO-8601), `source_run`, `sources[]` (each entry includes URL + capture date) — sufficient to pass AB#3144's validator without modification.
No new credential plumbing; auth flows through AB#3132's existing surface.
Tests cover deterministic path derivation, frontmatter shape, and storage-abstraction invocation against an in-memory backend.
description: Implement the writer step invoked on `keep`. Produces exactly one article in the sibling research repo via the storage abstraction from AB#3132: Johnny-Decimal path (`<area>/<category>/<id>-<slug>.md`) derived from the archivist's `johnny_decimal_hint` and the existing INDEX state, plus minimal frontmatter (`title`, `captured_at`, `source_run`, `sources[]`). Full frontmatter schema enforcement is AB#3144's job — this task emits a shape that AB#3144's validator will accept.
title: Sibling-repo writer over AB#3132 storage abstraction

acceptance_criteria: A `keep` decision results in both the article file AND an INDEX.md entry being written through the AB#3132 storage abstraction in the same logical write.
INDEX append is idempotent: re-running the same `keep` for an already-indexed article does not duplicate the entry.
Harness scenario exercises all three dispositions end-to-end (in-memory storage backend) and asserts: article presence/absence, INDEX state, and expand re-entry payload.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass.
description: Append the kept article to `INDEX.md` as part of the same write transaction that creates the article (task-3). This is an append-only first-appearance update; the deterministic full regenerator lives in AB#3147. Add the harness scenario that drives the full research sub-workflow end-to-end and asserts all three dispositions behave correctly, including the article + INDEX state after a `keep`.
title: `INDEX.md` append-on-write + end-to-end harness coverage
